name: internal-comms-community
description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal ...
capabilities: Generate 3P (Progress, Plans, Problems) team updates following structured formats
Create company-wide newsletters with proper tone and organization
Write FAQ responses with clear explanations
Draft status reports for leadership and stakeholders
Compose incident reports with technical accuracy
Format project updates with consistent structure
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 15 potential security patterns, all confirmed as false positives upon manual review. LICENSE.txt contains standard Apache 2.0 license text with legitimate URLs. SKILL.md references example file paths (e.g., examples/3p-updates.md) that were misidentified as shell commands. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, no network operations, and no security risks. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: Struggle to write internal updates in your company format? This skill provides ready-to-use templates for 3P updates, newsletters, status reports, and FAQs so you can communicate clearly and consistently.
userTitle: Write Internal Communications with Company Templates
limitations: Requires user to provide specific content about their situation
Templates may need customization for unique company cultures
Cannot access real company data or internal systems
User must verify accuracy before sending communications

useCases.title: Weekly Team Status Reports
useCases.description: Engineering leads write 3P updates to keep leadership aligned on team progress, upcoming plans, and blocking issues

useCases.targetUser: HR professionals and internal comms teams
useCases.title: Company-Wide Announcements
useCases.description: HR and communications teams draft newsletters and leadership updates to share company news with all employees

useCases.title: Incident Response Documentation
useCases.description: Site reliability engineers write structured incident reports to communicate what happened during outages

promptTemplates.title: Write a 3P Update
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a 3P update for my team this week. Progress: We launched the new authentication system. Plans: Next week we will add rate limiting. Problems: We're blocked on database migration approvals.

promptTemplates.title: Draft a Company Newsletter
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a company newsletter announcing our new product launch, two customer success stories, and upcoming team building events next month

promptTemplates.title: Answer Employee FAQ
promptTemplates.prompt: Write an FAQ response explaining the new remote work policy: employees can work from home 3 days per week, must be in office on Tuesdays and Thursdays for team collaboration

promptTemplates.title: Create Incident Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft an incident report for the 2-hour outage yesterday. Root cause was database connection pool exhaustion. Fix: Increased pool size. Prevention: Added monitoring alerts.

bestPractices: Provide specific details about your situation rather than vague requests
Review the generated communication against your company's actual guidelines before sending
Use this skill as a starting point and customize the tone for your organization
Include relevant data and metrics in progress reports for maximum impact
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to write sensitive HR actions like terminations or disciplinary notices
Do not share confidential company information or trade secrets in prompts
Do not rely solely on AI-generated content without human review for important communications
Do not use this skill for external customer communications or marketing materials

faq.question: What communication types can this skill help write?
faq.answer: This skill supports 3P updates (Progress, Plans, Problems), company newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my company's communication guidelines?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. The skill includes common formatting templates. For best results, describe any specific requirements your company has.

faq.question: Can this skill access my company's previous communications?
faq.answer: No. This skill cannot access your company data or past communications. You need to provide relevant context in your prompt.

faq.question: How do I get the best results from this skill?
faq.answer: Be specific about your situation. Include concrete details, metrics, and context rather than general requests.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates for my company?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the generated output as a starting point and modify it to match your company's tone and format preferences.

faq.question: Is this skill appropriate for sensitive communications?
faq.answer: Use your judgment. This skill works well for routine updates and documentation. For sensitive matters like layoffs, legal issues, or crisis communications, work directly with HR and legal teams.
